title: Training LLMs to be Better Text Embedders through Bidirectional Reconstruction
summary: Large language models (LLMs) have increasingly been explored as powerful text embedders. Existing LLM-based text embedding approaches often leverage the embedding of the final token, typically a reserved special token such as [EOS]. However, these tokens have not been intentionally trained to capture the semantics of the whole context, limiting their capacity as text embeddings, especially for retrieval and re-ranking tasks. We propose to add a new training stage before contrastive learning to e

title: incdec.py
summary:
for all your ++ -- needs 
 C-style increment and decrement operators for python. Note- using this will override the call x.pos.pos for x and x.neg.neg for --x. If for some reason you do want regular plusplus or negneg then just wrap it one level up like so --x. Also note the above doesn't apply to x, x--, as those regularly raise SyntaxError.
